name: Repost-with-agent
description: Skill-only OpenClaw/current-harness plugin for agent-driven, browser-based reposting (v4.5.8 — no CLI, no MCP server, no shell registration script). The running agent (OpenClaw via its built-in browser, Claude Code via chrome-devtools-mcp, or another explicit browser adapter) reads pair configs from ~/.repost-with-agent/pairs.json and uses its native tools to scrape sources, dedupe (Layer 1 string-match + Layer 2 agent semantic check), apply custom user skip rules, expand URLs without changing source wording, post via the user's logged-in browser, append history, manage source-item fanout manifests for scheduled backfills, and notify the user via the configured current-harness message route. NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES: public destination text must preserve original source wording exactly by default; compact/reword only if the live destination UI rejects or cuts off the exact cleaned draft for length, preserving intent, tone, links, key claims, and nuance. Successful source items MUST notify the user via configured delivery; source fanouts use one aggregate message with all destination outcomes. Silent publishes are a bug.
